name: ruby-dev
title: ruby-dev
summary: Header files for compiling extension modules for Ruby (default version)
description:
Header files for compiling extension modules for Ruby (default version)
Ruby is the interpreted scripting language for quick and easy
object-oriented programming.  It has many features to process text
files and to do system management tasks (as in perl).  It is simple,
straight-forward, and extensible.
.
This package contains the header files and the mkmf library, necessary
to make extension library for Ruby. It is also required to build
many gems.
.
This package is a dependency package, which depends on Debian's default Ruby
version (currently v3.3).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-dev because Header files for compiling extension modules for Ruby (default version).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
